title: Gritty Smoky Roasted Jamaican Rice Paella
description: A smoky, light Jamaican-inspired rice dish perfect for a sick day, featuring the perfect al-dente finish and low-fat content.

ingredients.name: uncooked long-grain rice
ingredients.notes: preferably jasmine rice or similar variety.

ingredients.name: grated fresh ginger
ingredients.notes: use a microplane or fine grater.

instructions: Heat the oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned, about 4 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken and let it rest, then chop it into bite-sized pieces.
Add the onion powder, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the diced tomatoes, grated ginger, and chili powder to the pan and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the rice to the pan and stir to coat the rice with the spice mixture.
Add 3 cups of water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan with a lid, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the paella with a fork and serve hot.
